data.policy_text: A regular monitoring program is key to managing risk in an organization.  Security monitoring occurs on both physical areas as well as logical components in different information system areas.  Information security monitoring confirms that appropriate mechanisms and controls are in place to secure systems and applications, that they are effective, and are not being bypassed in any way.
The purpose of this  Logging and Monitoring Policy ("Policy") is to define a framework for proactively monitoring events in order to detect potential security incidents quickly and efficiently.

This Policy applies to all employees and third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization's information and communications technology (ICT) systems. Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the 'Policy Exception Management procedure.’

shall develop, implement, and maintain a Logging and Monitoring Policy to secure the confidentiality, integrity, availability, secrecy, and privacy of information and its underlying assets for day-to-day business operations.

Logging requirements shall be determined based on scope, information to be captured in audit logs, frequency for review of audit log data, and applicable laws and regulations. High level log activities that the company would like to track shall be defined. Potential log sources shall be identified for log monitoring based upon a risk assessment and their importance to the business. The critical log sources shall be configured and documented for log generation. Logs generated for activities that affect the availability and security of the systems, shall be configured for monitoring.

Logs shall capture the below details - at minimum: User IDs. Date and Time of the event. Details of the event. Details of the Asset and its location where possible. For applications and  following log types shall be monitored - at minimum: Security Logs. Administrative and Operator Logs. Fault Logs. Backup logs.

Logs shall be stored in centralized log storage separate from the operational environment in which actual events were generated. Logs shall be configured in a way that does not affect system performance. Logging level shall be sufficient to audit individual accountability, reconstruct events, detect intrusions, identify problems and demonstrate regulatory compliance, as may be necessary. Logs shall be retained for a minimum period of 1 year, or as per the retention period defined by the business owner or in accordance with any legal & regulatory requirements.

Log reporting shall be performed to summarize significant activity over a particular period of time or to record detailed information related to a particular event or series of events. The reporting shall capture detail such as top 10 sources/targets, source countries, failed logins etc. Frequency of log reporting shall be determined based on the criticality of systems and audience.

Adequate security controls shall be taken into account throughout its lifecycle to protect its confidentiality, integrity and availability. Access to the logs shall be strictly controlled. No user shall be granted permission to modify the logs in an unauthorized manner. Log repository shall be hardened as per the applicable security baseline documents.

Monitoring performed by third parties shall involve Non-Disclosure Agreements (NDA) and Service Level Agreements (SLA). The SLA shall include procedures for log reporting and escalation. Logs generated and stored shall be monitored in real time by a central log monitoring system. The central log monitoring system shall be capable of centralization, sorting and parsing of the logs collected from different IT Systems to identify, contain and respond quickly to events/incidents in the network A dedicated monitoring team operating under IT Section shall be assigned to centrally monitor the logs generated by all the IT systems. Logs shall not be analyzed by the same individual whose activities are being logged and monitored, to ensure segregation of duties. Corrective and preventive measures on reported incidents shall be taken. Logs shall be backed up on a regular basis, in accordance with the log storage and retention requirements. The monitoring team shall, on a periodic basis, submit lo

All Employees and 'Third Parties' are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. In addition, violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the 's Human Capital Department, considering the recommendation of IS Team and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws, and regulations. Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record. Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the 'Policy Exception Management procedure.'

This Policy is developed and owned by the  IS Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO. IS Team shall initiate a review of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization's operating environment. Feedback, suggestions, or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the CISO at CISO@.net.

The Information Security Incident Management Policy outlines the overall framework for managing security incidents within the organization. It establishes roles and responsibilities for incident detection, response and recovery.

The purpose of this Information Security Incident Management Policy (“Policy”) is to ensure that information security incidents within the organization are communicated in a manner which allows timely corrective actions to be taken. Furthermore, the Policy establishes a commitment to comply with applicable sections of the federal and global laws and regulations, including but not limited to UAE Information Assurance Regulation, ISO27001 and National Cyber Risk Management Framework (NCRMF). The following are the key objectives of this Policy: Launch an incident response plan: Development, implementation, and maintenance of a plan to guide incident response activities. Establish a CSIRT: Establishment of a Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) in charge of the incident management and response plan. Enable reporting: Reporting information security incidents that results in damage or risk to information security assets and operations through appropriate management channels to st

This Policy applies to anybody involved in the security Incident management and/or response including but not limited to employees and critical third parties who access or manage information and information assets or use the organization's information and communications technology (ICT) systems.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the ‘Policy Exception Management procedure’.

<CLIENT> shall develop, implement, and maintain an incident management program where personnel shall report information security events and weaknesses, collect, and analyze evidence of security incidents, and ensure security incidents are adequately managed.

<CLIENT> shall: Identify personnel to form the proper CSIRT. Establish the CSIRT before developing the incident response plan. Ensure that one of the first responsibilities of CSIRT is to create the incident response plan. Ensure that the CSIRT must include members from the following teams, at a minimum: Team Leader – is usually senior manager responsible for managing incidents and directing actions to other team members. Boundary Protection Experts - usually individuals who are experts in logical perimeter security (expertise on Firewalls, IDS/IPS, WAF, Proxy etc.). Network Administrators. Physical Security. Legal Human Capital for incidents that was originated by an employee. Corporate Communications for managing the communication of incidents that has been revealed to public. The CSIRT shall: Develop incident management and Incident Response plan, process, and procedures. Response to information security incidents and minimise the impact. Investigate incidents and try to determine t

The CSIRT shall: Develop, implement, and maintain an incident response plan that includes the following, at a minimum: A roadmap and high-level approach for implementing organization’s incident response capability. A process to define reportable incidents and metrics for measuring the severity and Impact of incidents. Define the required resources and management support to effectively maintain and mature an incident response capability. Documentation that is reviewed and approved by defined personnel or roles. Distribute copies of the incident response plan to identified stakeholders. Update incident response plan regularly based on lessons learned. Communicate incident response plan changes to defined incident response personnel. Protect the incident response plan from unauthorised disclosure and modification. Perform investigations based on information distributed by an information sharing community to reduce the risks of similar incidents. Perform post-incident analysis to identify

The CSIRT shall: Define, classify, and prioritise incidents to help identify the impact and scope of the incident. Establish an incident classification scheme in line with the incident response plan considering regulatory issuances regarding incident management. Ensure that the SOC (Security Operations Centre) performs the following, at a minimum when an information security incident is reported: Assess the information security events using the agreed information security event and incident classification scale. Decide whether the events should be classified as information security incidents. Ensure that the assessment of the incident and the associated decision is forwarded to the CSIRT for confirmation and / or reassessment.

The CSIRT shall: Ensure that the incident response training provided by the organisation is linked to assigned roles and responsibilities of respective personnel to include appropriate content and level of detail in such training.

The CSIRT in coordination with other appropriate teams shall: Develop incident response testing procedures to: Determine the overall effectiveness of its incident response capabilities. Identify potential weaknesses or deficiencies. Ensure that the incident response testing approach simulates pre-defined breach scenarios across the incident response lifecycle including detection, reporting, and recovery to normal operations. Utilize checklists, table top (discussion-based) exercises, and functional (performance of duties in a simulated environment) exercises for incident response testing. Participate in sector, national, and international exercises to further test the organization’s incident response capabilities.

The CSIRT shall: Respond to information security incidents through a nominated point of contact and other relevant persons of the organization or external parties. Ensure that the response to incidents include the following activities, at a minimum: Collecting the evidence as soon as possible after the occurrence. Conducting information security forensics analysis, as required. Escalation, as required. Ensuring all involved response activities are properly logged for later analysis. Communicating the existence of the information security incident or any relevant details thereof to other internal and external people or organizations with a need-to-know. Dealing with information security weakness found to cause or contribute to the incident. Closing and recording the incident after successful resolution.
The CSIRT shall document Information security Incidents with reference to the <CLIENT> Information Security Incident Documentation Procedure.

The CSIRT shall: Implement relevant mechanisms to enable the types, volumes, costs (liaising with Finance Team), and impacts of information security incidents to be quantified and monitored. Utilize the information gained from evaluating information security incidents to identify recurring or high impact incidents and inform risk assessment and risk treatment activities.

<CLIENT> shall: Develop and follow internal procedures when dealing with evidence for the purposes of disciplinary and legal action. Include the following processes related to the collection of potential evidence for incidents, at a minimum: Identification process: the process involving the search for, recognition and documentation of potential evidence. Collection process: the process of gathering the physical items that can contain potential evidence.

data.section: Acquisition process: the process of creating a copy of data within a defined set.
data.policy_text: Preservation process: the process to maintain and safeguard the integrity and original condition of the potential evidence. Establish internal procedures to create the above processes in accordance with different types of media, devices, and state of devices (e.g., powered on or off state). Ensure that these procedures include the following, at a minimum: Chain of custody. Safety of evidence. Safety of personnel. Roles and responsibilities of personnel involved. Competency of personnel. Documentation. Briefing.

The CSIRT shall: Ensure priority information related to incidents are shared to relevant stakeholders in a timely manner even if it has not been fully analysed or resolved. Coordinate and share information in consultation with legal, with external entities, including, but not limited to external service providers, regulatory bodies, entities involved in the supply chain for organization’s information systems. Provide notice accordingly for all incidents involving Personally Identifiable Information (PII).

This Policy is to be part of all employees, contractors and third parties’ processes, and all are responsible for understanding and adhering to this Policy. Any violation of this Policy shall be reported to Policy Owner. Violation of this Policy shall be subjected to disciplinary action by the <CLIENT>'s Human Capital Department, taking into consideration the recommendation of the Information Security department and in accordance with other applicable policies, laws and regulations. Disciplinary action may range from a verbal warning to discontinuation of access to facility or services, or termination of employment/contract/agreement, and possible legal action. The actual action taken shall depend on factors such as the seriousness of the breach and the user's disciplinary record.

Any exceptions on the applicability of this Policy should be formally requested through the Policy Exception Management procedure.

This Policy is developed and owned by the <CLIENT> Information Security (IS) Team. It is reviewed by the CISO and approved by the CEO. <CLIENT> IS Team will initiate a review and of this Policy annually or whenever a significant change occurs in the organization’s operating environment. Feedback, suggestions or questions regarding the Policy shall be directed to the <CLIENT> IS Team at infosec@<CLIENT>.net.
